pattern_description: MULTIPLE CAUSAL PATHS: The cause (Marketing Spend) reaches the outcome (Sales) through both a direct effect and an indirect effect via a mediator (Brand Awareness). Tests whether the LLM can decompose total effect into direct and indirect components — a core skill in mediation analysis.
difficulty_notes: Medium. 4 nodes, 4 edges. Introduces the concept of partial mediation. A weak model will mention only one of the two paths or will fail to distinguish direct from indirect effects.
description: Marketing spend increases sales both directly (e.g. through promotions and discounts) and indirectly by building brand awareness, which in turn drives sales. Seasonality independently affects sales.

explanation_points: Marketing Spend affects Sales through two paths: a direct path and an indirect path through Brand Awareness
Brand Awareness is a partial mediator
Seasonality is an independent cause of Sales, unrelated to marketing
The total effect of Marketing on Sales is the sum of the direct effect plus the indirect effect via Brand Awareness

counterfactual.expected_outcome: Sales increase via two mechanisms: directly through immediate promotional effects, and indirectly through increased brand awareness over time. Seasonality is unaffected.
